report_name: Klue OAuth Integration Breach Exposes Salesforce Data in 'Icarus' Supply Chain Attack

threat_actors.name: Icarus
threat_actors.description: A newly emerged extortion group that claimed responsibility for the Klue supply chain attack. They harvested OAuth tokens to access and exfiltrate data from customer Salesforce and Gong environments.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attacker gained initial access to Klue's production environment using a compromised legacy credential.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The attacker modified Klue's trusted integration infrastructure by injecting malicious code, which was then used to attack Klue's customers.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The attacker's malicious code, injected into Klue's infrastructure, was designed to harvest OAuth 2.0 refresh and access tokens used for API integrations.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The Icarus group used stolen OAuth tokens to access and exfiltrate sensitive data such as contacts and sales communications from their victims' Salesforce and Gong tenants.

identities.name: Klue
identities.description: A market intelligence platform whose integration infrastructure was compromised in a supply chain attack, leading to the exposure of its customers' data.

identities.name: Huntress
identities.description: A Klue customer whose Salesforce and Gong data was exposed and exfiltrated by the Icarus group.

identities.name: Recorded Future
identities.description: A Klue customer whose Salesforce and Gong data was exposed and exfiltrated by the Icarus group.

identities.name: Tanium
identities.description: A Klue customer whose Salesforce and Gong data was exposed and exfiltrated by the Icarus group.

identities.name: Jamf
identities.description: A Klue customer whose Salesforce and Gong data was exposed and exfiltrated by the Icarus group.

identities.name: Sprout Social
identities.description: A Klue customer whose Salesforce and Gong data was exposed and exfiltrated by the Icarus group.

identities.name: Insurity
identities.description: A Klue customer whose Salesforce and Gong data was exposed and exfiltrated by the Icarus group.

infrastructure.name: Compromised Klue Integration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The attacker injected malicious code into Klue's production integration infrastructure to harvest OAuth tokens.

indicators.name: Anomalous Salesforce API Usage by OAuth Application
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Salesforce API Usage by OAuth Application
id: 7c8d3e9f-a1b2-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects anomalous API activity from an OAuth connected application in Salesforce, potentially indicating a compromised token or supply chain attack like the Klue 'Icarus' incident. This rule is a conceptual template based on hunting hints and should be tuned to baseline normal activity for each connected application.
references:
  - https://www.rescana.com/post/klue-oauth-integration-breach-exposes-salesforce-customer-data-in-icarus-supply-chain-attack
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1528
  - attack.t1530
level: medium
logsource:
  category: api
  service: salesforce
detection:
  selection:
    event_type: 'ApiTotalUsage'
    # This is a placeholder for a statistical threshold or anomaly detection engine.
    # A real implementation would require baselining API calls per app and user.
    # For example, alert if query_count > (average_query_count_for_app * 5)
    query_count: '>1000'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous API activity from an OAuth connected application in Salesforce, such as a high volume of 'describe' or 'query' calls, which could indicate a compromised token being used for data exfiltration.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Salesforce API Usage by OAuth Application
Anomalous Salesforce API Usage by OAuth Application
